parsed_review.summary: CP00-329 is a Risk-A, 10-unit descriptor-only intake pack covering the RP10.P05.M05 permission/audit-binding tail (9 units) plus the RP10.P05.M06 synthetic-fixture head (1 unit), range RP10.P05.M05.S14-RP10.P05.M06.S01. Coverage matches exactly across the closeout-pack plan, manifest, registry binding, contract projection, and prompt baseline: deliverable distribution {fixture:2,test:3,hermes_evidence:1,implementation:4} and micro-title {Permission And Audit Binding:9, Synthetic Fixture Set:1}, all RP10. The pack does NOT over-claim: manifest sets production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, status=in_progress; the only readiness signal is production_ready_candidate gated on coverage.valid && tail.valid (descriptor verification, not runtime). Grep found no "production_ready": true / "runtime_ready": true / enterprise-trust / Claude-final-approval over-claims in the contract or manifest. No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold at code level: packages/intake/src has no fs/network/child_process/eval calls, and row-extras enforce real_client_data_loaded=false, executes_unit_test_runtime_paths=false, executes_command_runtime=false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, cross_tenant_access_allowed=false. Claude stays read-only and not final approver across the descriptor authority_boundary, manifest, no-write attestation, and review-request (read_only=true, tools Read/Grep/Glob). Hermes is used only as a deterministic control-plane evidence/receipt pattern (receipt_shape ..._no_write, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false), not product runtime. Tests (3 CP329 blocks in model.test.js), the contract validator (deep-equals descriptor/requirements/attestation, asserts CP321-328 no-regression, historical tail = CP00-329), and the README#cp00-329 descriptor section are all consistent, and the readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1432-byte valid JSON, non-zero stdout). Two non-blocking P3 notes only. This read-only PASS does not by itself close the pack: the hardening sequence still requires receipt validation, finding adjudication, construction inspection, final closeout validation (the npm validate/test suite), and human final approval before any enterprise-trust/runtime claim.

findings.title: Post-review required pack artifacts not yet present (expected at this stage)
findings.details: manifest.required_pack_artifacts lists command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but only manifest.json currently exists under docs/closeout-packs/cp00-329/. This is consistent with the hardening_sequence: the current step is read_only_claude_run_raw_capture (step 3 of 9); those four artifacts are produced by the later normalize/finding_adjudication/construction_inspection/final_closeout_validation steps that consume this review. It is recorded only to make explicit that pack closeout remains gated on those post-review steps plus human final approval, not on this read-only PASS.
findings.recommendation: No code change. Ensure the remaining hardening steps generate the four required_pack_artifacts before commit, and confirm exactly one valid review receipt is recorded (pack_level_claude_review.valid_review_receipts is currently []).
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational/expected by design; non-blocking. Confirm artifacts are generated in subsequent hardening steps prior to closeout commit.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json mis-classifies the pack's own evidence dir as unrelated dirty
findings.details: changed-file-scope.json lists docs/closeout-packs/cp00-329/ (and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md) under both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved. The pack's own evidence directory docs/closeout-packs/cp00-329/ is a core required pack artifact, not an unrelated dirty file. The authoritative manifest.worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved correctly omits cp00-329/ (listing only the docx, new-session-handoff.md, ldip result, and workbook/), so the over-inclusive classification is confined to this generated scope helper and does not affect pack boundaries, the production_ready claim, or the changed-file scoping used for review (active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is []).
findings.recommendation: Adjust the changed-file-scope generator so the active pack's own evidence directory is not also emitted under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved; keep it solely under the pack/untracked-implementation classification for clarity.
findings.post_review_disposition: Minor generated-metadata hygiene nit; non-blocking. Manifest classification is authoritative and correct.

notes: CP00-329-F1 was resolved by generating command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json before final validation.
CP00-329-F2 is deferred as generated helper metadata hygiene; manifest.worktree_transition_policy remains the authoritative worktree classification and is correct.
No P0/P1/P2 findings were reported; neither P3 blocks pack or goal closeout.
